title: Structural Feasibility Confidence (SFC): From Engineering Confidence to Structural Feasibility Metrics and the Structural Boundaries of LLMs
description: Structural Feasibility Confidence (SFC) proposes structural feasibility as a foundational engineering principle connecting human engineering confidence, feasibility metrics, Large Language Model behavior, and future Structural Intelligence architectures. The repository develops SFC through three stages: Structural Feasibility Confidence, Structural Feasibility Metrics, and Structural Feasibility Scope. It argues that many capabilities of modern LLMs can be interpreted as implicit structural feasibility evaluation, while the Structural Feasibility Scope Principle (SFSP) clarifies the architectural boundary between implicit feasibility computation and systems requiring explicit structural representations, runtime state, structural memory, task graphs, differential structures, and persistent internal representations.
notes: This repository is part of the Structural Intelligence research series by Sizhe Tan. It introduces Structural Feasibility Confidence as a unifying engineering framework and highlights SFC-210 and SFC-210X as core contributions for interpreting LLMs as implicit structural feasibility engines and for defining the structural feasibility scope boundary of current and future AI architectures.
